user.title: Waveshare relay bus
user.description: How does Home Assistant reach the relay bus?

serial.title: Serial bus
serial.description: Select the USB / RS485 serial adapter your relay boards are wired to, and the bus baud rate (all boards on one bus share a baud rate).

network.title: Network bus
network.description: For HA in Docker (e.g. Docker Desktop on macOS, which can't pass USB through): point this at a socat/ser2net bridge on the machine with the adapter, or an RS485-to-Ethernet gateway. Use 'RTU over TCP' for socat/transparent gateways; 'Modbus TCP' only for an MBAP gateway.

device.title: Add a relay board
device.description: Enter the Modbus address of the board (factory default is 1) and how many relay channels it has. The board is tested before it is added.

error.cannot_connect: Could not open the bus. Check the serial port, or the bridge/gateway host and port.
error.no_response: No relay board answered at that address. Check the wiring, address, and baud rate.

abort.already_configured: This bus is already set up.

add_device.title: Add a relay board
add_device.description: Add another board on the same bus. Each board needs a unique Modbus address.

add_cover.title: Add a roller shutter
add_cover.description: Pair the up and down relays of one shutter into a single cover. The relay numbers match the switch labels (Relay 1, Relay 2, …). Leave the travel times at 0 to add it uncalibrated — open, close and stop still work; enter the seconds a full run takes each way to unlock the position slider.

error.no_response: No relay board answered at that address.
error.duplicate_address: A board with that address is already configured.
error.same_channel: The up and down relays must be different channels.
error.channel_out_of_range: A relay number is outside this board's channel count.
error.channel_in_use: One of those relays is already paired to another shutter.

abort.no_boards: Add a relay board before adding a shutter.
abort.no_covers: There are no roller shutters to remove.

pulse.name: Pulse relay
pulse.description: Momentarily switch one or more relays, then return them to rest.

duration.name: Duration
duration.description: How long to hold the active state, in seconds.

invert.name: Invert
invert.description: Pulse OFF then back ON instead of ON then OFF.

all_on.name: All relays on
all_on.description: Switch every relay on a board on in a single bus transaction.

all_off.name: All relays off
all_off.description: Switch every relay on a board off in a single bus transaction.

set_device_address.name: Set device (Modbus) address
set_device_address.description: Write a board's Modbus address (one board on the bus at a time).

set_baud_rate.name: Set baud rate
set_baud_rate.description: Write a board's RS485 baud rate.

blueprint_user_modified.title: A newer version of your customized blueprint is available

init.title: Customized blueprint — keep yours or take the update?
init.description:
You edited the blueprint at `{path}`, and this update ships a newer version. Your automations and their inputs are unaffected either way.

- **Take the new version** — replace your file with the latest; your current file is backed up next to it as `*.bak`.
- **Keep my version** — leave your edited file as-is; you won't be reminded again until an even newer version ships.

Tip: to keep customizing *and* always get updates, copy the blueprint to your own name/path and build automations from that copy — a private copy is never touched.

menu_options.take_new: Take the new version (back up mine)
